evals.prompt: I need to squash the last 3 commits on my feature branch into one before a PR. My global git config has commit.gpgsign = true. Show me the exact commands.
evals.expected_output: Uses git reset --soft HEAD~3 + git commit (not git commit-tree). Does not suggest git rebase -i. Mentions GPG signing is preserved.

assertions.name: uses-git-commit
assertions.description: Uses git commit for the final squash

evals.prompt: My last commit shows as 'Unverified' on GitHub even though commit.gpgsign is true. I used git commit-tree to rewrite its parent SHA. How do I fix it?
evals.expected_output: Diagnoses git commit-tree as the cause, suggests git commit --amend -S or git rebase --exec to re-sign, explains why plumbing bypasses signing.

assertions.name: diagnoses-commit-tree
assertions.description: Identifies git commit-tree as the cause

assertions.name: suggests-resign
assertions.description: Suggests git commit --amend -S or rebase --exec to re-sign

assertions.name: no-commit-tree-again
assertions.description: Does not suggest git commit-tree in the fix

evals.prompt: I want to add a Refs: trailer to the last 5 commits on a project where commit.gpgsign = true. What's the non-interactive approach that keeps signatures valid?
evals.expected_output: Suggests git rebase --exec with git commit --amend -S. Does not use git rebase -i or git commit-tree. Includes proper Assisted-by trailer in examples.
